gamma.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_openalex invocation=tool/openalex_bridge.hexa (real fetch deferred to sub-agent or OFFLINE-FALLBACK)

kernel_steps.1_intake_topic: keybindings.ts port ROI — evaluate whether any structural reshape (drop legacy shim / split per-feature / lift-to-JSON-SSOT) yields net-positive return on investment. Bounded by hive-tui base + coding-agent extension as currently vendored from pi-mono e71b47a9.
kernel_steps.2_pre_register_falsifiers: F1..F7 enumerated below before any candidate scoring — falsifiers fixed in advance, verdicts attached after evaluation.
kernel_steps.3_fan_out_axes: K=3 declared orthogonal axes (design-strategy / falsifier-coverage / cost-vs-value) — pairwise conceptual correlation matrix below.
kernel_steps.4_gate_tier1: Promote candidate to Tier-1 iff ≥3 falsifiers PASS and 0 falsifiers FAIL. UNCERTAIN allowed but downgrades to conditional Tier-1.
kernel_steps.5_emit_witness: this file at /Users/ghost/core/nexus/design/kick//2026-04-26_keybindings-ts-port-roi_omega_cycle.json + sentinel line on stdout.

coding_agent_extension.extension_strategy: TS declaration merging into @need-singularity/hive-tui Keybindings interface (lines 60-62)

legacy_shim.purpose: rewrite legacy short names (cursorUp, expandTools, ...) → namespaced ids (tui.editor.cursorUp, app.tools.expand, ...)
legacy_shim.consumer_surface: internal only (migrateKeybindingsConfig + isLegacyKeybindingName, both within same file) — grep confirms zero external imports
legacy_shim.vendoring_origin: inherited via commit 81d9c3be4 vendor pi-mono packages/coding-agent @ e71b47a9; carried forward unchanged through hard-fork ca802a6ae

in_source_key_collisions.policy: context-scoped — different screens may reuse the same chord; TuiKeybindingsManager.rebuild() only flags user-config collisions, never definition-level overlaps
in_source_key_collisions.interpretation: intentional ambiguity resolved by active-screen routing — NOT a defect surface for any port

candidate_ports.name: drop legacy KEYBINDING_NAME_MIGRATIONS shim
candidate_ports.axis_design_strategy: carryover-eviction (vendored migration shim from pi-mono retired once user base migrated)
candidate_ports.delta_summary: delete lines 219-280 + isLegacyKeybindingName + migrateKeybindingsConfig branch + simplify keybindings-migration.test.ts (3 cases → 1 'no-op-migration' regression test)

axis_cost_vs_value.value_capability_unlocked: removes two-name lookup confusion in toKeybindingsConfig pipeline; eliminates silent inheritance from upstream pi-mono shape

candidate_ports.blast_radius: low — single TS file + 3 test cases; no cross-package consumer of KEYBINDING_NAME_MIGRATIONS

candidate_ports.name: split coding-agent KEYBINDINGS into per-feature modules (session/, models/, tree/, message/)
candidate_ports.delta_summary: create src/core/keybindings/{base,session,models,tree,message,clipboard,editor}.ts; original keybindings.ts becomes barrel re-export; each feature module declares-merge into hive-tui Keybindings

axis_cost_vs_value.value_capability_unlocked: smaller blast-radius diffs per feature owner; clearer ownership signal — but no defect prevention

candidate_ports.blast_radius: medium — 7+ new files, declaration-merging across files can fragment registry view in IDE; ESM ordering must guarantee all merges visible at consumer site

candidate_ports.name: lift KEYBINDINGS defaults to keybindings.defaults.json (cross-runtime SSOT)
candidate_ports.axis_design_strategy: data-driven SSOT with codegen for TS types
candidate_ports.delta_summary: extract KEYBINDINGS object → keybindings.defaults.json + add codegen step (e.g., scripts/gen-keybindings-types.ts) → emit src/core/keybindings.gen.ts; nexus/hexa side could read same JSON for parity

axis_cost_vs_value.value_capability_unlocked: nexus/hexa cross-runtime parity (potentially) + doc generation no longer needs TS parser; BUT loses platform-conditional ergonomics

candidate_ports.blast_radius: high — codegen pipeline + build-step + JSON schema + handling for process.platform conditionals (would require runtime overlay or Mustache-style templating)

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: legacy-non-break
pre_registered_falsifiers.predicate: removing KEYBINDING_NAME_MIGRATIONS does not silently lose user bindings for any in-the-wild legacy keybindings.json
pre_registered_falsifiers.evidence_required: telemetry / opt-in ping or git-blame of last legacy-name commit > 12 months ago

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: conflict-non-worse
pre_registered_falsifiers.predicate: post-split in-source key collision count ≤ pre-split (9 observed)

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: platform-conditional-encoding
pre_registered_falsifiers.predicate: static JSON encoding preserves process.platform conditional defaults without runtime overlay layer

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: line-count-cap
pre_registered_falsifiers.predicate: chosen port net diff ≤ +50 LOC (cycle prefers reductive ports)

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: idempotence-under-repeat
pre_registered_falsifiers.predicate: running the port action twice has no divergent effect
pre_registered_falsifiers.evidence_required: deletion is trivially idempotent; verify migration codepath fallback

F1.evidence: no telemetry available in repo; git-blame of legacy short-name introduction predates vendoring (e71b47a9, 81d9c3be4 inherits without origin record); cannot rule out pre-vendoring users with stale keybindings.json. Recommendation: schedule eviction one cycle out, after either (a) telemetry instrumented or (b) explicit deprecation cycle posted.

F4.evidence: estimated net = -58 LOC, well below +50 cap (and net negative)

F6.evidence: lines_removed (62) > lines_added (4); ROI signed-positive on LOC alone; capability: removes cross-name lookup ambiguity

F7.evidence: deletion idempotent by construction; post-port migrateKeybindingsConfig becomes near-identity (only orderKeybindingsConfig + drop-on-overlap retained)

PORT_A.tier: Tier-1-conditional (gated on F1 evidence next cycle)

F2.evidence: no realization to scan; cannot verify in advance

F6.evidence: no defect prevention demonstrated; capability is ownership-signal sugar; LOC delta negative

F7.evidence: split is structural one-shot, idempotence trivially holds

PORT_B.tier: rejected — F4 hard-fail blocks Tier-1 gate

F3.evidence: process.platform === 'win32' conditionals on app.suspend + app.clipboard.pasteImage cannot be encoded in static JSON without a runtime overlay or templating layer; introducing either reverses the SSOT rationale

F4.evidence: estimated net = +140 LOC including codegen scaffolding

F6.evidence: cross-runtime parity unlocked in principle but no consumer is currently asking for it (no nexus keybindings.* glob hits; nexus side has no keybindings concept at all)

F7.evidence: codegen is idempotent if cache key correct

PORT_C.tier: rejected — F3 + F4 double-fail; defer indefinitely until cross-runtime consumer materializes

tier1_promotions.gating_falsifier: F1 (legacy-non-break) — UNCERTAIN until telemetry or 12-month deprecation cycle elapses
tier1_promotions.recommended_action: ScheduleWakeup / schedule a follow-up cycle in ~30 days (or after first user touches keybindings config UI) to re-evaluate F1 with usage signal. If still UNCERTAIN at that point, post explicit deprecation note in the next vendoring sync, then eject one cycle later.

rejected_candidates.reason: fails F4 (line-count cap +140 > +50); no defect-prevention payoff
fails F3 (platform-conditional encoding) + F4 (LOC cap); no current cross-runtime consumer to amortize codegen pipeline

ROI_meta_finding.headline: Of 3 fanned-out keybindings.ts ports, only PORT_A (drop vendored legacy migration shim) clears the falsifier pattern, and only conditionally. PORT_B (per-feature split) and PORT_C (JSON SSOT) both fail the line-count cap; PORT_C additionally fails the platform-conditional encoding falsifier. The honest answer is: keybindings.ts is at a local optimum for current consumers — only the legacy shim represents free-money once usage telemetry confirms it.
ROI_meta_finding.PORT_A_eventual_ROI_estimate: +58 LOC reclaimed + 1 cognitive-load surface eliminated, conditional on F1 evidence cycle
ROI_meta_finding.PORT_B_ROI_estimate: negative (-140 LOC delta, no defect/capability win)
ROI_meta_finding.PORT_C_ROI_estimate: negative without external cross-runtime consumer; would invert if nexus/hexa adopts a TUI surface
